paper_title: A Structural Ranking of Economic Complexity
research_answer: The structural approach and the original ECI agree quite strongly at the country level. The rank correlation between the structural OLS-based ranking and the original ECI is 0.96, indicating broad agreement. When comparing two variants of the structural approach itself — one estimated with OLS and one with PPML — the correlation rises above 0.995, meaning the choice of gravity model estimator makes very little practical difference. So while the structural method has a stronger theoretical foundation, it largely validates the ordering produced by the original ECI at the country level.
supporting_quotes: The rank correlation with the one derived from using PPML in the first step is larger than .995, and even with the original ECI that starts from a binary country-product matrix indicating country-product pairs with RCA of at least one it has a rank correlation of .96.

data_points.metric: Rank correlation: structural OLS vs structural PPML
Rank correlation: structural OLS vs original ECI
